results.rendered_html:
hreṣ (cf. √1. heṣ) cl. 1. Ā. ( Dhātup. xvi, 21 ) hreṣate (mc. also P. ˚ti; pf. jihreṣe &c. Gr.),
to neigh (as a horse), whinny,  MBh. ;  Kāv.  &c.;
to go, move,  Dhātup. xvi, 18  (v.l. for preṣ) :
Caus. hreṣayati, to cause to neigh,  MBh.
